volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Stichus (Section 5.6.766 - Section 5.7.775)

sections.label: Section 5.6.766
sections.original: dare amicum amicae ? euge euge , sic furi datur .
sections.translation: Bravo ! bravo ! that ’ s the way it ’ s given to a thief [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.6.767
sections.original: Age , iam infla buccas , nunciam aliquid suaviter .
sections.translation: Come , blow out your cheeks now ; something in the amorous way [*] at once . Give us a new tune in return for the old wine .

sections.label: Section 5.7.769
sections.original: qui Iónicus aut cinaédicust , qui hoc tále facere póssiet ?
sections.translation: What person in the Ionian [*] or the ballet line is there that can do anything like that ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.770
sections.original: Si istóc me vorsu víceris , alió me provocáto :
sections.translation: If you get the better of me this turn [*] , just challenge me to another .

sections.label: Section 5.7.771
sections.translation: Just you do it in this fashion .

sections.label: Section 5.7.772
sections.original: Nunc páriter ambo . omnís voco cinaédos contra .
sections.translation: Now , then , both in the same step . ( They dance quietly , in the same measure .) I challenge all the dancing-masters to dance against me . ’ Tis no more possible for there to be enough of this for us than for there to be too much rain for a mushroom .

sections.label: Section 5.7.774
sections.original: Intro hínc abeamus nunciam : saltátum satis pro vinost .
sections.translation: Let ’ s away hence in-doors at once now ; we ’ ve danced long enough for the wine [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.7.775
sections.original: vos , spectatores , plaudite atque ite ad vos comissatum .
sections.translation: You , Spectators , give us your applause , and then go home to enjoy yourselves .
